pattern: Each output cell is just the maximum over a fixed three-by-three window, so a sliding pass across every interior center fills the smaller grid.
hints: The result has side n - 2 because each cell is centered on an interior position.
For output cell (i, j) the window spans rows i..i+2 and columns j..j+2 of the original grid.
Iterate i and j over the reduced range and take the max of the nine surrounding values for each.

edge_inputs.description: minimum 3x3 grid yields a single cell
max value sits in the corner of one window
single high value covered by every window
max in one window not shared by the neighbour window

stress.description: random square grid near the size cap to stress every window
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100))
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
